name: qh3

metadata.name: qh3
metadata.summary: A lightway and fast implementation of QUIC and HTTP/3

documentation:
qh3
===

|pypi-pyversions| |pypi-stats|

.. |pypi-pyversions| image:: https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/qh3.svg
    :target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/qh3
    :alt: Supported Interpreters

.. |pypi-stats| image:: https://img.shields.io/pypi/dm/qh3
   :target: https://pypistats.org/packages/qh3
   :alt: PyPI - Downloads

What is ``qh3``?
----------------

``qh3`` is a maintained fork of the ``aioquic`` library.

It is lighter, faster, and more adapted to a broader audience as this package has no external dependency
and does not rely on mainstream OpenSSL.

While it is a compatible fork, it is not a drop-in replacement since the first major. See the CHANGELOG for details.

``qh3`` is a library for the QUIC network protocol in Python. It features
a minimal TLS 1.3 implementation, a QUIC stack, and an HTTP/3 stack.

QUIC was standardized in `RFC 9000`_ and HTTP/3 in `RFC 9114`_.
``qh3`` follow the standardized version of QUIC and HTTP/3.

QUIC stack conforming with `RFC 9000`_ (QUIC v1) and `RFC 9369`_ (QUIC v2)

To learn more about ``qh3`` please `read the documentation`_.

``qh3`` stands for **Q** UIC . **H** TTP/ **3**.

Our primary goal with this fork is mainly about the client aspect, while the
server side code is maintained, we do not have enough time to add feature to it at the moment.

PR are welcomed for any improvement (server or client).

Why should I use ``qh3``?
-----------------------------

``qh3`` has been designed to be embedded into Python client and server
libraries wishing to support QUIC and/or HTTP/3. The goal is to provide a
common codebase for Python libraries in the hope of avoiding duplicated effort.

Both the QUIC and the HTTP/3 APIs follow the "bring your own I/O" pattern,
leaving actual I/O operations to the API user. This approach has a number of
advantages including making the code testable and allowing integration with
different concurrency models.

This library is the lowest level you can find for handling QUIC and HTTP/3. Here are higher libraries:

- mid-way: `urllib3.future`_
- highest and easiest: `niquests`_ (Recommended!)

Features
--------

- QUIC stack conforming with `RFC 9000`_
- HTTP/3 stack conforming with `RFC 9114`_
- minimal TLS 1.3 implementation conforming with `RFC 8446`_
- IPv4 and IPv6 support
- connection migration and NAT rebinding
- logging TLS traffic secrets
- logging QUIC events in QLOG format
- HTTP/3 server push support
- Post-Quantum (KEM) Key-Exchange (Kyber R3 NIST)

Requirements
------------

``qh3`` requires Python and PyPy 3.7 or greater.

Running the examples
--------------------

`qh3` comes with a number of examples illustrating various QUIC use cases.

You can browse these examples here: https://github.com/jawah/qh3/tree/main/examples

License
-------

``qh3`` is released under the `BSD license`_.

.. _read the documentation: https://qh3.readthedocs.io/en/latest/
.. _BSD license: https://qh3.readthedocs.io/en/latest/license.html
.. _RFC 8446: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8446
.. _RFC 9000: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9000
.. _RFC 9114: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9114
.. _RFC 9369: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9369
.. _niquests: https://github.com/jawah/niquests
.. _urllib3.future: https://github.com/jawah/urllib3.future

functions.name: qh3.asyncio.server.serve
documentation:
Start a QUIC server at the given `host` and `port`.

{func}`serve` requires a {class}`~qh3.quic.configuration.QuicConfiguration`
containing TLS certificate and private key as the `configuration` argument.

{func}`serve` also accepts the following optional arguments:

- `create_protocol` allows customizing the {class}`~asyncio.Protocol` that
  manages the connection. It should be a callable or class accepting the same
  arguments as {class}`~qh3.asyncio.QuicConnectionProtocol` and returning
  an instance of {class}`~qh3.asyncio.QuicConnectionProtocol` or a subclass.
- `session_ticket_fetcher` is a callback which is invoked by the TLS
  engine when a session ticket is presented by the peer. It should return
  the session ticket with the specified ID or `None` if it is not found.
- `session_ticket_handler` is a callback which is invoked by the TLS
  engine when a new session ticket is issued. It should store the session
  ticket for future lookup.
- `retry` specifies whether client addresses should be validated prior to
  the cryptographic handshake using a retry packet.
- `stream_handler` is a callback which is invoked whenever a stream is
  created. It must accept two arguments: a {class}`asyncio.StreamReader`
  and a {class}`asyncio.StreamWriter`.

classes.name: examples.http3_server.SessionTicketStore
documentation: Simple in-memory store for session tickets.

functions.name: qh3.asyncio.client.connect
documentation:
Connect to a QUIC server at the given `host` and `port`.

{meth}`connect()` returns an awaitable. Awaiting it yields a
{class}`~qh3.asyncio.QuicConnectionProtocol` which can be used to
create streams.

{func}`connect` also accepts the following optional arguments:

- `configuration` is a {class}`~qh3.quic.configuration.QuicConfiguration`
  configuration object.
- `create_protocol` allows customizing the {class}`~asyncio.Protocol` that
  manages the connection. It should be a callable or class accepting the same
  arguments as {class}`~qh3.asyncio.QuicConnectionProtocol` and returning
  an instance of {class}`~qh3.asyncio.QuicConnectionProtocol` or a subclass.
- `session_ticket_handler` is a callback which is invoked by the TLS
  engine when a new session ticket is received.
- `stream_handler` is a callback which is invoked whenever a stream is
  created. It must accept two arguments: a {class}`asyncio.StreamReader`
  and a {class}`asyncio.StreamWriter`.
- `local_port` is the UDP port number that this client wants to bind.

The StopSendingReceived event is fired when the remote peer requests

classes.name: qh3.quic.events.StreamReset
documentation: The StreamReset event is fired when the remote peer resets a stream.

Check connection is established even in the client's INITIAL is lost.

The client's PTO fires, triggering retransmission.

methods.name: tests.test_connection.QuicConnectionTest.test_connect_with_loss_2
documentation:
Check connection is established even in the server's INITIAL is lost.

The client receives HANDSHAKE packets before it has the corresponding keys
and decides to retransmit its own CRYPTO to speedup handshake completion.

methods.name: tests.test_connection.QuicConnectionTest.test_connect_with_loss_3
documentation:
Check connection is established even in the server's INITIAL + HANDSHAKE are
lost.

The server receives duplicate CRYPTO and decides to retransmit its own
CRYPTO to speedup handshake completion.

methods.name: tests.test_connection.QuicConnectionTest.test_connect_with_loss_4
documentation: Check connection is established even in the server's HANDSHAKE is lost.

methods.name: tests.test_connection.QuicConnectionTest.test_connect_with_loss_5
documentation: Check connection is established even in the server's HANDSHAKE_DONE is lost.

methods.name: qh3.quic.stream.QuicStreamReceiver._pull_data
documentation: Remove data from the front of the buffer.

The receive part of a QUIC stream.

It finishes:
- immediately for a send-only stream
- upon reception of a STREAM_RESET frame
- upon reception of a data frame with the FIN bit set

It finishes:
- immediately for a receive-only stream
- upon acknowledgement of a STREAM_RESET frame
- upon acknowledgement of a data frame with the FIN bit set

classes.name: qh3.h3.events.H3Event
documentation: Base class for HTTP/3 events.

classes.name: qh3.h3.events.DataReceived
documentation:
The DataReceived event is fired whenever data is received on a stream from
the remote peer.

classes.name: qh3.h3.events.DatagramReceived
documentation:
The DatagramReceived is fired whenever a datagram is received from the
the remote peer.

classes.name: qh3.h3.events.InformationalHeadersReceived
documentation:
This event is fired whenever an informational response has been caught inflight!
The stream cannot be ended there.

classes.name: qh3.h3.events.HeadersReceived
documentation: The HeadersReceived event is fired whenever headers are received.

classes.name: qh3.h3.events.PushPromiseReceived
documentation:
The PushedStreamReceived event is fired whenever a pushed stream has been
received from the remote peer.

classes.name: qh3.h3.events.WebTransportStreamDataReceived
documentation:
The WebTransportStreamDataReceived is fired whenever data is received
for a WebTransport stream.

classes.name: qh3.h3.exceptions.H3Error
documentation: Base class for HTTP/3 exceptions.

classes.name: qh3.h3.exceptions.NoAvailablePushIDError
documentation: There are no available push IDs left.

classes.name: qh3.h3.connection.ProtocolError
documentation:
Base class for protocol errors.

These errors are not exposed to the API user, they are handled
in {meth}`H3Connection.handle_event`.

classes.name: qh3.h3.connection.H3Connection

methods.name: qh3.h3.connection.H3Connection.create_webtransport_stream
documentation:
Create a WebTransport stream and return the stream ID.

```
param session_id

The WebTransport session identifier.

param is_unidirectional

Whether to create a unidirectional stream.
```

methods.name: qh3.h3.connection.H3Connection.handle_event
documentation:
Handle a QUIC event and return a list of HTTP events.

```
param event

The QUIC event to handle.
```

methods.name: qh3.h3.connection.H3Connection.send_datagram
documentation:
Send a datagram for the specified flow.

```
param flow_id

The flow ID.

param data

The HTTP/3 datagram payload.
```

methods.name: qh3.h3.connection.H3Connection.send_push_promise
documentation:
Send a push promise related to the specified stream.

Returns the stream ID on which headers and data can be sent.

```
param stream_id

The stream ID on which to send the data.

param headers

The HTTP request headers for this push.
```

methods.name: qh3.h3.connection.H3Connection.send_data
documentation:
Send data on the given stream.

To retrieve datagram which need to be sent over the network call the QUIC
connection's {meth}`~qh3.connection.QuicConnection.datagrams_to_send`
method.

```
param stream_id

The stream ID on which to send the data.

param data

The data to send.

param end_stream

Whether to end the stream.
```

methods.name: qh3.h3.connection.H3Connection.send_headers
documentation:
Send headers on the given stream.

To retrieve datagram which need to be sent over the network call the QUIC
connection's {meth}`~qh3.connection.QuicConnection.datagrams_to_send`
method.

```
param stream_id

The stream ID on which to send the headers.

param headers

The HTTP headers to send.

param end_stream

Whether to end the stream.
```

methods.name: qh3.h3.connection.H3Connection.received_settings
documentation: Return the received SETTINGS frame, or None.

methods.name: qh3.h3.connection.H3Connection.sent_settings
documentation: Return the sent SETTINGS frame, or None.

methods.name: qh3.h3.connection.H3Connection._create_uni_stream
documentation: Create an unidirectional stream of the given type.

methods.name: qh3.h3.connection.H3Connection._decode_headers
documentation:
Decode a HEADERS block and send decoder updates on the decoder stream.

This is called with frame_data=None when a stream becomes unblocked.

methods.name: qh3.h3.connection.H3Connection._encode_headers
documentation: Encode a HEADERS block and send encoder updates on the encoder stream.

methods.name: qh3.h3.connection.H3Connection._get_local_settings
documentation: Return the local HTTP/3 settings.

methods.name: qh3.h3.connection.H3Connection._handle_control_frame
documentation: Handle a frame received on the peer's control stream.

methods.name: qh3.h3.connection.H3Connection._handle_request_or_push_frame
documentation: Handle a frame received on a request or push stream.

methods.name: qh3.h3.connection.H3Connection._receive_datagram
documentation: Handle a datagram.

methods.name: qh3.h3.connection.H3Connection._receive_request_or_push_data
documentation: Handle data received on a request or push stream.

A low-level HTTP/3 connection object.

```
param quic

A  instance.
```

functions.name: qh3.tls.utcnow
documentation: facilitate mocking for the test suite

functions.name: qh3.tls.load_pem_private_key
documentation: Load a PEM-encoded private key.

documentation: Load a chain of PEM-encoded X509 certificates.

Context manager to push a variable-length block, with `capacity` bytes

LISTS

callback types

methods.name: qh3.tls.Context.session_resumed
documentation: Returns True if session resumption was successfully used.

functions.name: qh3.quic.connection.is_version_compatible
documentation:
Return whether it is possible to perform compatible version negotiation
from `from_version` to `to_version`.

functions.name: qh3.quic.connection.stream_is_client_initiated
documentation: Returns True if the stream is client initiated.

functions.name: qh3.quic.connection.stream_is_unidirectional
documentation: Returns True if the stream is unidirectional.

variables.name: qh3.quic.connection.MAX_PENDING_CRYPTO
documentation: in bytes

documentation: FIXME: this is arbitrary!

variables.name: qh3.quic.connection.APPLICATION_CLOSE_FRAME_CAPACITY

classes.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection.change_connection_id
documentation:
Switch to the next available connection ID and retire
the previous one.

After calling this method call {meth}`datagrams_to_send` to retrieve data
which needs to be sent.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection.close
documentation:
Close the connection.

```
param error_code

An error code indicating why the connection is
being closed.

param reason_phrase

A human-readable explanation of why the
connection is being closed.
```

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection.connect
documentation:
Initiate the TLS handshake.

This method can only be called for clients and a single time.

After calling this method call {meth}`datagrams_to_send` to retrieve data
which needs to be sent.

```
param addr

The network address of the remote peer.

param now

The current time.
```

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection.datagrams_to_send
documentation:
Return a list of `(data, addr)` tuples of datagrams which need to be
sent, and the network address to which they need to be sent.

After calling this method call {meth}`get_timer` to know when the next
timer needs to be set.

```
param now

The current time.
```

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection.get_next_available_stream_id
documentation: Return the stream ID for the next stream created by this endpoint.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection.get_timer
documentation: Return the time at which the timer should fire or None if no timer is needed.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection.handle_timer
documentation:
Handle the timer.

After calling this method call {meth}`datagrams_to_send` to retrieve data
which needs to be sent.

```
param now

The current time.
```

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection.next_event
documentation:
Retrieve the next event from the event buffer.

Returns `None` if there are no buffered events.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection.receive_datagram
documentation:
Handle an incoming datagram.

After calling this method call {meth}`datagrams_to_send` to retrieve data
which needs to be sent.

```
param data

The datagram which was received.

param addr

The network address from which the datagram was received.

param now

The current time.
```

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection.request_key_update
documentation: Request an update of the encryption keys.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection.reset_stream
documentation:
Abruptly terminate the sending part of a stream.

```
param stream_id

The stream's ID.

param error_code

An error code indicating why the stream is being reset.
```

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection.send_ping
documentation:
Send a PING frame to the peer.

```
param uid

A unique ID for this PING.
```

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection.send_datagram_frame
documentation:
Send a DATAGRAM frame.

```
param data

The data to be sent.
```

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection.send_stream_data
documentation:
Send data on the specific stream.

```
param stream_id

The stream's ID.

param data

The data to be sent.

param end_stream

If set to , the FIN bit will be set.
```

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection.stop_stream
documentation:
Request termination of the receiving part of a stream.

```
param stream_id

The stream's ID.

param error_code

An error code indicating why the stream is being stopped.
```

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._alpn_handler
documentation:
Callback which is invoked by the TLS engine at most once, when the
ALPN negotiation completes.

At this point, TLS extensions have been received so we can parse the
transport parameters.
Private

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._assert_stream_can_receive
documentation: Check the specified stream can receive data or raises a QuicConnectionError.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._assert_stream_can_send
documentation: Check the specified stream can send data or raises a QuicConnectionError.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._consume_peer_cid
documentation:
Update the destination connection ID by taking the next
available connection ID provided by the peer.

documentation: Begin the close procedure.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._close_end
documentation: End the close procedure.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._connect
documentation: Start the client handshake.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._get_or_create_stream
documentation: Get or create a stream in response to a received frame.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._get_or_create_stream_for_send
documentation:
Get or create a QUIC stream in order to send data to the peer.

This always occurs as a result of an API call.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._handle_ack_frame
documentation: Handle an ACK frame.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._handle_connection_close_frame
documentation: Handle a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._handle_crypto_frame
documentation: Handle a CRYPTO frame.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._handle_data_blocked_frame
documentation: Handle a DATA_BLOCKED frame.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._handle_datagram_frame
documentation: Handle a DATAGRAM frame.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._handle_handshake_done_frame
documentation: Handle a HANDSHAKE_DONE frame.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._handle_max_data_frame
documentation:
Handle a MAX_DATA frame.

This adjusts the total amount of we can send to the peer.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._handle_max_stream_data_frame
documentation:
Handle a MAX_STREAM_DATA frame.

This adjusts the amount of data we can send on a specific stream.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._handle_max_streams_bidi_frame
documentation:
Handle a MAX_STREAMS_BIDI frame.

This raises number of bidirectional streams we can initiate to the peer.

documentation:
Handle a MAX_STREAMS_UNI frame.

This raises number of unidirectional streams we can initiate to the peer.

documentation: Handle a NEW_CONNECTION_ID frame.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._handle_new_token_frame
documentation: Handle a NEW_TOKEN frame.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._handle_padding_frame
documentation: Handle a PADDING frame.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._handle_path_challenge_frame
documentation: Handle a PATH_CHALLENGE frame.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._handle_path_response_frame
documentation: Handle a PATH_RESPONSE frame.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._handle_ping_frame
documentation: Handle a PING frame.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._handle_reset_stream_frame
documentation: Handle a RESET_STREAM frame.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._handle_retire_connection_id_frame
documentation: Handle a RETIRE_CONNECTION_ID frame.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._handle_stop_sending_frame
documentation: Handle a STOP_SENDING frame.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._handle_stream_frame
documentation: Handle a STREAM frame.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._handle_stream_data_blocked_frame
documentation: Handle a STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED frame.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._handle_streams_blocked_frame
documentation: Handle a STREAMS_BLOCKED frame.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._log_key_retired
documentation: Log a key retirement.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._log_key_updated
documentation: Log a key update.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._on_ack_delivery
documentation: Callback when an ACK frame is acknowledged or lost.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._on_connection_limit_delivery
documentation: Callback when a MAX_DATA or MAX_STREAMS frame is acknowledged or lost.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._on_handshake_done_delivery
documentation: Callback when a HANDSHAKE_DONE frame is acknowledged or lost.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._on_max_stream_data_delivery
documentation: Callback when a MAX_STREAM_DATA frame is acknowledged or lost.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._on_new_connection_id_delivery
documentation: Callback when a NEW_CONNECTION_ID frame is acknowledged or lost.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._on_ping_delivery
documentation: Callback when a PING frame is acknowledged or lost.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._on_retire_connection_id_delivery
documentation: Callback when a RETIRE_CONNECTION_ID frame is acknowledged or lost.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._payload_received
documentation: Handle a QUIC packet payload.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._receive_retry_packet
documentation: Handle a retry packet.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._receive_version_negotiation_packet
documentation:
Handle a version negotiation packet.

This is used in "Incompatible Version Negotiation", see:
https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9368#section-2.2

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._replenish_connection_ids
documentation: Generate new connection IDs.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._retire_peer_cid
documentation: Retire a destination connection ID.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._parse_transport_parameters
documentation:
Parse and apply remote transport parameters.

`from_session_ticket` is `True` when restoring saved transport parameters,
and `False` when handling received transport parameters.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._update_traffic_key
documentation:
Callback which is invoked by the TLS engine when new traffic keys are
available.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._write_connection_limits
documentation: Raise MAX_DATA or MAX_STREAMS if needed.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._write_datagram_frame
documentation:
Write a DATAGRAM frame.

Returns True if the frame was processed, False otherwise.

methods.name: qh3.quic.connection.QuicConnection._write_stream_limits
documentation:
Raise MAX_STREAM_DATA if needed.

The only case where `stream.max_stream_data_local` is zero is for
locally created unidirectional streams. We skip such streams to avoid
spurious logging.

A QUIC connection.

The state machine is driven by three kinds of sources:

- the API user requesting data to be send out (see {meth}`connect`,
  {meth}`reset_stream`, {meth}`send_ping`, {meth}`send_datagram_frame`
  and {meth}`send_stream_data`)
- data being received from the network (see {meth}`receive_datagram`)
- a timer firing (see {meth}`handle_timer`)

```
param configuration

The QUIC configuration to use.
```
